mechanism_type: condensation of 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds, aldehydes, and ammonia through enamine and Knoevenagel-type intermediates
common_conditions: combine carbonyl components with ammonia or ammonium source under heating or Dean-Stark-type conditions to generate a 1,4-dihydropyridine that may be oxidized to a pyridine
description_short: Classical multicomponent condensation used to access 1,4-dihydropyridines and, after oxidation, substituted pyridines.

transformation_type: palladium-catalyzed arylation or alkenylation of olefins
mechanism_type: Pd(0)/Pd(II) catalytic cycle involving oxidative addition, migratory insertion, and beta-hydride elimination
common_conditions: combine an aryl or vinyl halide/triflate with an alkene in the presence of a palladium catalyst, phosphine ligand, base, and heat to generate a substituted alkene
description_short: Foundational palladium-catalyzed coupling of unsaturated halides or pseudohalides with olefins to form substituted alkenes.

transformation_type: iodide-mediated rearrangement or ring expansion of activated aziridines
mechanism_type: nucleophilic opening of N-acylaziridines followed by intramolecular substitution to oxazolines, thiazolines, or related heterocycles
common_conditions: treat an activated aziridine with iodide or thiocyanate under mild heating or reflux to induce nucleophilic opening and ring expansion to a five-membered heterocycle
description_short: Aziridine rearrangement family that converts N-acylaziridines into oxazolines and related heterocycles under nucleophilic conditions.

mechanism_type: halogenation of enolized acyl halides generated from carboxylic acids and phosphorus halides
common_conditions: convert the carboxylic acid to an acyl halide with phosphorus halide, halogenate at the alpha position, then hydrolyze or trap the alpha-halo acyl halide with a nucleophile
description_short: Classical alpha-halogenation chemistry of carboxylic acids proceeding through in situ generated acyl halides.

mechanism_type: base-promoted addition of nitroalkanes or nitronates to aldehydes or ketones followed by protonation of beta-nitro alkoxides
common_conditions: deprotonate a nitroalkane under basic conditions and add to an aldehyde or ketone to furnish a beta-nitro alcohol that may be elaborated further
description_short: Carbon-carbon bond-forming addition of nitroalkanes to carbonyl compounds to furnish beta-nitro alcohols and related products.
